title: Indulgent Peruvian Fish Ceviche
description: A modern take on the classic Peruvian dish, featuring light rum, agemono-deep-fried onions, and a hint of herbal flavors.
instructions: Combine lime juice, aji amarillo pepper, garlic, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
Add the sliced red onion and red bell pepper to the bowl and mix well.
Add the chopped cilantro to the bowl and mix well.
Cut the fish into small pieces and add to the bowl, making sure they are fully coated with the marinade.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours.
Heat the olive oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions to the pan and deep-fry until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the onions from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Add the fried onions to the bowl with the ceviche and mix well.
Strain the marinade from the bowl and discard the solids.
Add the strained marinade back to the bowl and mix well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for an additional 15 minutes, or up to 30 minutes.
Serve the ceviche in glasses or small bowls, garnished with a sprinkle of chopped cilantro and a few slices of fried onion.
